content:
# Functions

Block of reusable code to perform a specific action.

### Reusing Code

Using an existing code without writing it every time we need.

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/aa844695-76a0-4eef-ad78-a8184853263b.png)


#### Code

```python
def greet():
    print("Hello")

name = input()
print(name)
```

#### Input

```

Teja
```

#### Output

```

Teja
```

### Defining a Function

Function is uniquely identified by the `function_name`

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/48867533-bc9e-45e5-9d94-22a077274bbc.png)


#### Code

```python
def greet():
    print("Hello")

name = input()
print(name)
```

#### Input

```

Teja
```

#### Output

```

Teja
```

### Calling a Function

The functional block of code is executed only when the function is called.

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/84ce001f-f30a-4f10-a5ef-d2c2faa29eae.png)


#### Code

```python
def greet():
    print("Hello")

name = input()
greet()
print(name)
```
  

#### Input

```

Teja
```

#### Output

```
Hello
Teja
```


### Defining & Calling a Function

A function should be defined before it is called.

#### Code

```python
name = input()
greet()
print(name)

def greet():
    print("Hello")
```

#### Input

```

Teja
```

#### Output

```

NameError: name 'greet' is not defined
```

### Printing a Message

Consider the following scenario, we want to create a function, that prints a custom message, based on some variable that is defined outside the function. In the below code snippet, we want to access the value in the variable `name` at line 2 in place of the `?`. 

#### Code

```python
def greet():
    msg = "Hello " + ? 
    print(msg)

name = input()
greet()
```

#### Input

```

Teja
```

#### Desired Output

```

Hello Teja
```
We use the concept of Function Arguments for these types of scenarios.

### Function With Arguments

We can pass values to a function using an Argument.

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/2ebf11e4-d01b-4605-a6c3-4447ec7d75ac.png)

#### Code

```python
def greet(word):
    msg = "Hello " + word
    print(msg)

name = input()
greet(word=name)
```

#### Input

```

Teja
```

#### Output

```

Hello Teja
```

### Variables Inside a Function

A variable created inside a function can only be used in it.

#### Code

```python
def greet(word):
    msg = "Hello " + word

name = input()
greet(word=name)
print(msg)
```

#### Input

```

Teja
```

#### Output


```

NameError: name 'msg' is not defined
```

## Returning a Value

To return a value from the function use `return` keyword.
  
![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/e951094a-3007-4bff-b2b8-2ce521dc72e7.png)

Exits from the function when return statement is executed.

#### Code

```python
def greet(word):
    msg = "Hello " + word
    return msg

name = input()
greeting = greet(word=name)
print(greeting)
```

#### Input

```

Teja
```

#### Output

```

Hello Teja
```

Code written after `return` statement will not be executed.

#### Code

```python
def greet(word):
    msg = "Hello "+word
    return msg
    print(msg)

name = input()
greeting = greet(word=name)
print(greeting)
```

#### Input

```

Teja
```

#### Output

```

Hello Teja
```

### Built-in Functions

We are already using functions which are pre-defined in Python.
Built-in functions are readily available for reuse

 -  `print()`
 - `int()`
 - `str()`
 - `len()`
